request.prompt:
You are a precise data extraction algorithm. Analyze the following content regarding a strike from "ATAC". IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS: 1. Decide if this document is actually announcing a new/upcoming strike. - If it is a cancellation/revocation of a strike, set isStrike to false. - If it is just providing information about participation (adesione) of a strike that already happened, set isStrike to false. - If it is providing real-time service updates (e.g., metro is open/closed during the strike), set isStrike to false. - ONLY set isStrike to true if it is an announcement of an upcoming strike. 2. If isStrike is true, extract the strike details: - Dates must be in 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' format. - Guaranteed times must be arrays of 'HH:mm-HH:mm' (omitted if not specified). - Location codes should be: 2-digit ISTAT for regions (e.g., "03" for Lombardia), 2-letter for provinces (e.g., "MI" for Milan), or omitted for national strikes. Example Output if NOT a strike: { "isStrike": false } Example Output if IS a strike: { "isStrike": true, "strikeData": { "startDate": "2024-12-31 08:30:00", "endDate": "2024-12-31 12:30:00", "locationType": "REGION", "locationCodes": ["12"], "guaranteedTimes": ["06:00-09:00", "18:00-21:00"] } } Input Content (Pre-processing: dom-distillation):

Context Metadata

  • Page Title: Sciopero del trasporto pubblico, chiusa la metro A - Roma Servizi per la Mobilità

Sciopero del trasporto pubblico, chiusa la metro A

Pubblicato il: 07/07/2023 - Aggiornato il: 07/07/2023

status: 1 È in corso lo sciopero nazionale del trasporto pubblico di 24 ore. Ecco la situazione sulla rete capitolina: chiusa la metro A; possibili rallentamenti sulla metro B/B1 e sulla linea Termini-Centocelle; regolare la metro C; possibili riduzioni di corse per bus e tram.

response.thoughts:
**Analyzing a Real-Time Public Transport Strike Update**

Okay, so I've been given a snippet from "Sciopero del trasporto pubblico, chiusa la metro A - Roma Servizi per la Mobilità." The content tells me about a national public transport strike that's currently underway – a 24-hour affair, apparently. It details the impact in Rome: Metro A is closed, B/B1 and the Termini-Centocelle line might be slow, Metro C is unaffected, and bus and tram services are likely reduced.

The context is crucial here. The phrasing, "È in corso lo sciopero..." – "is in progress" – clearly indicates this is a status report on an *existing* strike, not a heads-up about a future one. My task is to ascertain whether this is an *announcement* of a future strike. Given the real-time nature of the update, with specific line statuses and the explicit mention that the strike "is in progress," the conclusion is straightforward. I need to set `isStrike` to false because this is not an announcement of an upcoming event, but a report on a situation unfolding *right now*. So, `isStrike` will be false.
